titleStmt.title: Letter from Frank Quinlan to Fr Provincial Thomas V. Nolan, 6 November 1918
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Frank Quinlan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Frank Quinlan to Fr Provincial Thomas V. Nolan (1867- 1922) regarding the delayed response from the War Office.Fr. Thomas Nolan became the Provincial of the Irish Province of the Society of Jesus from 1912 to 1922. Besides that, he was a Distribution Committee member responsible for the welfare and distribution of the Belgian refugees who had found refuge in Ireland on account of the World War I.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
68 Middle Abbey Street, Dublin 6 th Nov. 1918
P.F. QUINLAN, LL.B.,
Solicitor.
Major Rev. J. Fitzgibbon deed. Dear Fr Nolan I am sure you are wondering why I
have not sent you word with reference to the
above matter. I regret that I have had no
further word from the War Office and am
posting them a letter tonight asking for in-
formation. I do not wish to go to the expense
of a corrective affidavit by taking out Probate
at once as the amount is so small in the
Schedule of Assets. I am only to anxious to push the matter
on, but I suggest that I wait for a reply to my
letter of today to the War Office. Yours very sincerely Frank Quinlan Rev. T.V. Nolan S.J. Presbytery.
